# Backend — Ticket Management System

Node.js/Express REST API with TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and JWT authentication.

## Setup

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Copy environment config
cp .env.example .env

# Start development server (requires PostgreSQL running)
npm run dev
```

Server runs at `http://localhost:3001`. Swagger docs at `http://localhost:3001/api-docs`.

## Scripts

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start dev server with hot-reload |
| `npm run build` | Compile TypeScript to dist/ |
| `npm start` | Run compiled production build |
| `npm test` | Run tests |
| `npm run test:integration` | Run integration tests |
| `npm run lint` | Run ESLint |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── config/         # Environment, database, swagger config
├── controllers/    # Request handlers
├── middleware/     # Auth, authorization, validation, error handling
├── models/         # TypeScript interfaces and types
├── repositories/   # Database queries
├── routes/         # Express route definitions + validation schemas
└── services/       # Business logic
database/
└── migrations/     # SQL schema (001_initial_schema.sql)
```

## API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Role |
|--------|------|------|------|
| POST | /api/auth/login | No | — |
| GET | /api/auth/me | Yes | Any |
| PATCH | /api/auth/me | Yes | Any |
| GET | /api/tickets | Yes | Any |
| POST | /api/tickets | Yes | admin, user |
| GET | /api/tickets/:id | Yes | Any |
| PATCH | /api/tickets/:id | Yes | admin, user |
| PATCH | /api/tickets/:id/status | Yes | admin, user |
| GET | /api/tickets/:id/comments | Yes | Any |
| POST | /api/tickets/:id/comments | Yes | admin, user |
| GET | /api/users | Yes | Any |
| POST | /api/users | Yes | admin |
| PATCH | /api/users/:id | Yes | admin |
| DELETE | /api/users/:id | Yes | admin |
| GET | /api/roles | Yes | admin |
| POST | /api/roles | Yes | admin |
| PATCH | /api/roles/:id | Yes | admin |
| DELETE | /api/roles/:id | Yes | admin |

## Environment Variables

See [.env.example](.env.example) for all required variables.

# Frontend — Ticket Management System

React 18 SPA with TypeScript, Vite, and role-based access control.

## Setup

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Copy environment config
cp .env.example .env

# Start development server
npm run dev
```

App runs at `http://localhost:5173`.

## Scripts

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start Vite dev server |
| `npm run build` | Production build to dist/ |
| `npm run preview` | Preview production build |
| `npm test` | Run Vitest tests |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── components/     # Reusable UI components (common/, Layout, Modals)
├── context/        # React contexts (Auth, Toast)
├── hooks/          # Custom hooks (useRole)
├── pages/          # Route pages (Dashboard, Tickets, Kanban, Users, Roles)
├── services/       # API client and service modules
├── styles/         # Global CSS
└── types/          # TypeScript type definitions
```

## Features

- **Dashboard** — Ticket statistics and charts
- **Ticket List** — Paginated, searchable, filterable ticket table
- **Kanban Board** — Drag-and-drop ticket status management
- **Ticket Detail** — View, edit, status transitions, comments
- **User Management** — Admin CRUD for users (role assignment)
- **Role Management** — Admin CRUD for roles with screen permissions matrix
- **Authentication** — JWT login with protected routes
- **RBAC** — Admin (full), User (read/write tickets), Agent (read-only)

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Description | Default |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `VITE_API_URL` | Backend API base URL | `http://localhost:3001/api` |

tool_input.oldString:
# Support Ticket Management System

A full-stack internal support ticket management application with a React frontend, Node.js/Express backend, and PostgreSQL database.

## Features

- **Ticket CRUD** — Create, view, update tickets with priority and assignee
- **Edit Ticket Flow** — Dedicated edit page with pre-filled form and validation
- **Status State Machine** — Enforced lifecycle transitions (Open → In Progress → Resolved → Closed)
- **Kanban Board** — Drag-and-drop tickets between status columns
- **Comments** — Add discussion to tickets
- **Search & Filter** — Keyword search and status filtering
- **Dashboard** — Ticket statistics and resolved counts by period

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Vite, @dnd-kit |
| Backend | Node.js, Express, TypeScript, Zod |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
| Testing | Jest + Supertest (backend), Vitest + React Testing Library (frontend) |
| Containerization | Docker + docker-compose |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+
- Docker & Docker Compose
- PostgreSQL 16 (or use Docker)

### 1. Clone & Configure

```bash
# Backend environment
cp backend/.env.example backend/.env

# Frontend environment
cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env
```

### 2. Start Database (Docker)

```bash
docker-compose up db -d
```

This creates the database, runs migrations, and seeds sample data.

### 3. Backend

```bash
cd backend
npm install
npm run dev
```

Backend runs at `http://localhost:3001`.

### 4. Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

Frontend runs at `http://localhost:5173`.

### 5. Full Stack (Docker)

```bash
docker-compose up -d
```

All services start together. Frontend at `:5173`, Backend at `:3001`, DB at `:5432`.

## Running Tests

### Backend Integration Tests

```bash
cd backend
npm run test:integration
```

Tests cover:
- State machine transitions (all valid + invalid)
- Ticket CRUD operations
- Comment operations

### Frontend Tests

```bash
cd frontend
npm test
```

## API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/tickets` | List tickets (search, filter, paginate) |
| POST | `/api/tickets` | Create ticket |
| GET | `/api/tickets/:id` | Get ticket details |
| PATCH | `/api/tickets/:id` | Update ticket fields |
| PATCH | `/api/tickets/:id/status` | Change status (state machine enforced) |
| GET | `/api/tickets/:id/comments` | List comments |
| POST | `/api/tickets/:id/comments` | Add comment |
| GET | `/api/tickets/stats/status-counts` | Status distribution |
| GET | `/api/tickets/stats/resolved?period=week\|month\|year` | Resolved count |
| GET | `/api/users` | List users |
| GET | `/api/health` | Health check |

## Project Structure

```
ticket-management-system/
├── frontend/           # React application
│   └── src/
│       ├── components/ # UI components (common/ for reusable controls)
│       ├── pages/      # Page-level views
│       ├── services/   # API client layer
│       ├── types/      # TypeScript interfaces
│       └── styles/     # CSS design tokens & globals
├── backend/            # Node.js API
│   └── src/
│       ├── controllers/
│       ├── services/
│       ├── repositories/
│       ├── middleware/
│       ├── models/     # Types + state machine
│       └── routes/
├── database/           # SQL scripts
│   ├── migrations/
│   └── seeds/
├── docs/               # Documentation
└── docker-compose.yml
```

## Status State Machine

```
Open → In Progress → Resolved → Closed
Open → Cancelled
In Progress → Cancelled
```

Invalid transitions are rejected with HTTP 422.

tool_response:
I've now read every file. Here is the complete performance optimization analysis:

---

## Performance Optimization Analysis

### 1. Unnecessary Re-renders (Missing `useMemo`/`useCallback`)

**[KanbanBoard.tsx](frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx#L30-L35)** — `ticketsByStatus` is recomputed on every render via `.filter()` inside a `.reduce()`. This creates new array references every render, causing all `KanbanColumn` children to re-render even if `tickets` hasn't changed. Should be wrapped in `useMemo(…, [tickets])`.

**[KanbanBoard.tsx](frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx#L29)** — `sensors` from `useSensors(useSensor(...))` is recreated every render. Should be hoisted outside the component or memoized.

**[TicketDetailPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/TicketDetailPage.tsx#L62-L78)** — `handleStatusChange` and `handleAddComment` are recreated every render. Neither is wrapped in `useCallback`. `getAssigneeName()` and `getCreatorName()` are called inline during render — should be `useMemo` values.

**[TicketListPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/TicketListPage.tsx#L67-L78)** — Five handler functions (`handleSearchChange`, `handleStatusChange`, `handlePriorityChange`, `handleAssigneeChange`, `handleSortChange`) are recreated every render. Each calls `setPage(1)` plus a filter setter. These should be `useCallback`.

**[SearchFilter.tsx](frontend/src/components/SearchFilter.tsx#L54-L60)** — `statusOptions`, `priorityOptions`, and `assigneeOptions` are derived from static maps/props but are recreated as new arrays on every render. Should use `useMemo`.

**[TicketForm.tsx](frontend/src/components/TicketForm.tsx#L62-L66)** — `priorityOptions` and `userOptions` are recreated on every render from props. Should use `useMemo(…, [users])`.

**[CommentList.tsx](frontend/src/components/CommentList.tsx#L19-L21)** — `getUserName` is recreated every render. Should be `useCallback(…, [users])` or a `useMemo` lookup map.

**[AuthContext.tsx](frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx#L57)** — The context value object `{ user, isAuthenticated, loading, login, logout, updateUser }` is a **new object on every render**, which forces all consumers of `useAuth()` to re-render. Must wrap in `useMemo(…, [user, loading, login, logout, updateUser])`.

**[ToastContext.tsx](frontend/src/context/ToastContext.tsx#L30)** — Same issue: `{ showToast }` context value is a new object each render. Should use `useMemo(…, [showToast])`.

**[useRole.ts](frontend/src/hooks/useRole.ts#L5-L13)** — Returns a new object every call. Every component using `useRole()` gets a fresh object reference per render. Should wrap return in `useMemo`.

**[RolesPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/RolesPage.tsx#L36-L84)** — `handleCreate`, `handleEdit`, `handleDelete`, `handleSave` all recreated every render.

**[DashboardPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx#L42-L44)** — `totalTickets` is recalculated on every render. Minor, but should be `useMemo`.

---

### 2. Missing `React.memo` on Components

None of the components use `React.memo`. The highest-impact candidates:

| Component | Why it matters |
|-----------|---------------|
| `TicketCard` | Rendered in lists (Kanban columns). New `listeners`/`attributes` from `useDraggable` might already cause re-renders, but `React.memo` with custom comparator would help for non-drag renders. |
| `KanbanColumn` | 5 columns re-render on every parent state change (e.g., `activeTicket` change). |
| `StatusBadge` / `PriorityBadge` | Pure presentational, rendered in large tables/lists. |
| `Badge`, `Button`, `Card`, `Input`, `Select`, `TextArea`, `LoadingSpinner` | All common components are pure presentational — wrapping them in `React.memo` prevents re-renders when parent re-renders with same props. |
| `SearchFilter` | Receives 10+ props, many are callbacks that are currently recreated. |
| `CommentList` | Receives `comments[]` and `users[]` arrays. |
| `ToastItem` | Rendered in a list. Already internally pure. |

---

### 3. Redundant State

**[DashboardPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx#L13-L15)** — Three separate `useState` calls for `resolvedWeek`, `resolvedMonth`, `resolvedYear`. These are always fetched and set together. Should be a single state object:
```ts
const [resolvedCounts, setResolvedCounts] = useState({ week: 0, month: 0, year: 0 });
```
This reduces 3 state updates (and 3 re-renders in dev strict mode) to 1.

**[ProfilePage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx#L20)** — A single `loading` state is shared between `handleProfileSubmit` and `handlePasswordSubmit`. If both forms could theoretically submit concurrently, they'd interfere. Should have `profileLoading` and `passwordLoading` separately.

**[SearchFilter.tsx](frontend/src/components/SearchFilter.tsx#L46) + [TicketListPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/TicketListPage.tsx#L27)** — `search` is duplicated: `TicketListPage` owns `search` state and passes it as `initialSearch` to `SearchFilter`, which also maintains its own internal `search` state. This is a controlled/uncontrolled hybrid. The internal state in `SearchFilter` is redundant — it should be fully controlled by the parent, or the parent shouldn't hold the state.

**[RoleFormModal.tsx](frontend/src/components/RoleFormModal.tsx#L35-L37)** — Internal state mirrors the `role` prop via `useEffect`. This "derived state from props" anti-pattern can cause stale/sync bugs. A `key` prop on the modal (e.g., `key={role?.id ?? 'new'}`) would be cleaner.

---

### 4. API Calls That Could Be Parallelized / Cached

**Already parallelized (good):**
- `DashboardPage` — `Promise.all` for 4 stats calls
- `EditTicketPage` — `Promise.all` for ticket + users  
- `TicketDetailPage` — `Promise.all` for ticket + comments + users
- `UsersPage` — `Promise.all` for users + roles

**Duplicate calls across pages — missing cache:**
`userService.getAll()` is called independently by **5 pages**: `CreateTicketPage`, `EditTicketPage`, `TicketDetailPage`, `TicketListPage`, `UsersPage`. Navigating between these pages triggers redundant network requests for the same user list. Solutions:
- A `UsersContext` / shared cache hook
- React Query / SWR for automatic caching + deduplication
- A simple in-memory cache with TTL in `userService`

**[RolesPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/RolesPage.tsx#L35)** — Calls `api.get('/roles')` directly instead of going through a service. This is inconsistent with the service pattern used everywhere else, and prevents any future caching layer.

**[CreateTicketPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/CreateTicketPage.tsx#L13)** and **[TicketDetailPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/TicketDetailPage.tsx#L17)** — Both hardcode `CURRENT_USER_ID` instead of using `useAuth()`. This means comments and tickets created always use the demo user, not the logged-in user.

---

### 5. Components That Could Be Lazy-loaded

**[App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx#L1-L19)** — All 10 page components are eagerly imported. Since each is a distinct route, they're ideal for `React.lazy`:

```tsx
const DashboardPage = lazy(() => import('./pages/DashboardPage').then(m => ({ default: m.DashboardPage })));
const TicketListPage = lazy(() => import('./pages/TicketListPage').then(m => ({ default: m.TicketListPage })));
// etc.
```

Priority lazy-load candidates (by bundle weight):
1. **`KanbanPage`** — imports `@dnd-kit/core` (heavy DnD library), only used on this single route
2. **`RolesPage`** — admin-only, most users never visit
3. **`UsersPage`** — admin-only
4. **`ProfilePage`** — rarely visited
5. **`CreateTicketPage`** / **`EditTicketPage`** — write-only flows

The `LoginPage` should remain eagerly loaded since it's the entry point for unauthenticated users. `DashboardPage` can also stay eager as it's the default landing route.

A `<Suspense fallback={<LoadingSpinner />}>` wrapper around the `<Routes>` or individual routes would handle the loading states.

---

### 6. Test Structure and Improvements

**Current state:** Only **1 test file** exists for the entire frontend: [EditTicketPage.test.tsx](frontend/src/pages/EditTicketPage.test.tsx). Test setup is minimal ([setup.ts](frontend/src/test/setup.ts) only imports `@testing-library/jest-dom`).

**Issues with the existing test:**
- **Missing `ToastProvider` wrapper** — `EditTicketPage` calls `useToast()` which throws without `ToastProvider`. The test likely doesn't test toast messages, or it silently ignores the error.
- **Missing `AuthProvider` wrapper** — The component doesn't use auth directly, but if any child imports `useAuth`, it would break.
- **Only tests happy path** — No tests for: error states (API failure), loading states, cancel button behavior, missing ticket ID, validation errors.
- **No accessibility assertions** — No `toBeAccessible()` or axe-core checks.

**Missing test coverage (no test files exist for):**

| Category | Missing Tests |
|----------|---------------|
| Pages | `DashboardPage`, `TicketListPage`, `TicketDetailPage`, `CreateTicketPage`, `KanbanPage`, `LoginPage`, `ProfilePage`, `RolesPage`, `UsersPage` |
| Components | `CommentList`, `KanbanBoard`, `KanbanColumn`, `Layout`, `TicketCard`, `TicketForm`, `SearchFilter`, `StatusBadge`, `RoleFormModal`, `ProtectedRoute`, `AdminRoute`, `WriterRoute` |
| Common | `Badge`, `Button`, `Card`, `Input`, `Select`, `TextArea`, `Toast`, `LoadingSpinner` |
| Services | `ticket.service`, `user.service`, `comment.service`, `auth.service`, `error-utils`, `api` (interceptors) |
| Hooks | `useRole` |
| Context | `AuthContext` (login/logout flow), `ToastContext` |

**Recommended test priorities:**
1. `AuthContext` — core auth flow, token handling, redirect logic
2. `api.ts` interceptors — 401 redirect, error normalization  
3. `TicketListPage` — most complex page (filters, pagination, debounce)
4. `KanbanBoard` — drag-and-drop state transitions
5. Route guards (`ProtectedRoute`, `AdminRoute`, `WriterRoute`)

---

### Summary of Highest-Impact Fixes

| Priority | Issue | Impact |
|----------|-------|--------|
| **P0** | `AuthContext` value not memoized | Every state change re-renders **all** consumers app-wide |
| **P0** | `ToastContext` value not memoized | Same — cascading re-renders on every toast |
| **P0** | `useRole` returns new object every render | Re-renders every component using `canWrite`/`isAdmin` |
| **P1** | Lazy-load route pages (especially KanbanPage with `@dnd-kit`) | Reduces initial bundle size significantly |
| **P1** | `useMemo` for `ticketsByStatus` in `KanbanBoard` | Prevents 5 columns × N cards re-rendering on unrelated state changes |
| **P1** | `React.memo` on `TicketCard`, `KanbanColumn`, `StatusBadge` | Prevents list re-renders |
| **P2** | Cache/deduplicate `userService.getAll()` across pages | Eliminates 4+ redundant API calls per session |
| **P2** | `useCallback` for handler functions in `TicketListPage`, `TicketDetailPage` | Prevents unnecessary child re-renders |
| **P2** | Consolidate `DashboardPage` resolved stats into single state | Reduces 3 state updates to 1 |
| **P3** | Fix `SearchFilter` dual state / controlled-uncontrolled hybrid | Prevents state sync bugs |
| **P3** | Replace hardcoded `CURRENT_USER_ID` with `useAuth()` | Functional bug, not just perf |

## ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

### Backend Architecture
- **Layered**: Controllers → Services → Repositories → Database
- **Auth**: JWT-based (8h expiration)
- **Roles**: 3 built-in (admin, agent, user) + custom roles with permissions
- **Validation**: Zod schemas at route level
- **Error Handling**: Custom AppError with status codes and detailed messages
- **State Machine**: Ticket status transitions validated at service level

### Frontend Architecture
- **React 18**: Functional components with hooks
- **Context API**: Auth and Toast global state
- **React Router**: Client-side routing with route guards
- **Axios**: HTTP client with interceptors for JWT and 401 handling
- **Drag & Drop**: dnd-kit for Kanban functionality
- **Styling**: CSS modules for component scoping
- **Code Splitting**: Lazy-loaded page components

---
